TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Giɫgiɫaw of Vaɫulu village

S.FORM: izua za mareqali.
S.PHON: izua za marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: izua za mareqali.
S.PHON: izua za marəqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: There were two (young) friends.

S.FORM: "ari kikasivi, qalian," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: "ari kikasivi, qaɭian," aja tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: "ari kikasivi, qalian," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: "ari kikasivi, qaɭian," aja tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: They said to one another: “Let’s go and collect wood, friend.”

S.FORM: ka zemuker i vavua patsun ta za kamaya na qaliqali.
S.PHON: ka zəmukər i vavua paʦun ta za kamaja na qaɭiqaɭi.
S.FORM: ka zemuker i vavua pacun ta za kamaya na qaliqali.
S.PHON: ka zəmukər i vavua paʦun ta za kamaja na qaɭiqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: When they got to the fields, they saw some mango trees belonging to other people.

S.FORM: malulum anga.
S.PHON: maɭuɭum aŋa.
S.FORM: malulum anga.
S.PHON: maɭuɭum aŋa.
S.TRANSL: The fruit was ripe,

S.FORM: manu sauram aza mareqali.
S.PHON: manu sauram aza marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: manu sauram aza mareqali.
S.PHON: manu sauram aza marəqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: so the friends wanted to eat it.

S.FORM: manu vaik a tsemakaw.
S.PHON: manu vaik a ʦəmakaw.
S.FORM: manu vaik a cemakaw.
S.PHON: manu vaik a ʦəmakaw.
S.TRANSL: So they went to steal it,

S.FORM: ɫevavaw ta za kamaya a mareqali.
S.PHON: ʎəvavaw ta za kamaja a marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: ljevavaw ta za kamaya a mareqali.
S.PHON: ʎəvavaw ta za kamaja a marəqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: and got up in the mango tree,

S.FORM: keman izua i vavaw.
S.PHON: kəman izua i vavaw.
S.FORM: keman izua i vavaw.
S.PHON: kəman izua i vavaw.
S.TRANSL: and ate mangoes up there.

S.FORM: ka masilanga tiamadju, "ari, qalian, vaiki anga.
S.PHON: ka masiɭaŋa tiamaɉu, "ari, qaɭian, vaiki aŋa.
S.FORM: ka masilanga tiamadju, "ari, qalian, vaiki anga.
S.PHON: ka masiɭaŋa tiamaɉu, "ari, qaɭian, vaiki aŋa.
S.TRANSL: When they had had enough, they said: “Come on, friend, let’s go.

S.FORM: marekutj itjen.
S.PHON: marəkuc icən.
S.FORM: marekutj itjen.
S.PHON: marəkuʦ iʦən.
S.TRANSL: We’re afraid,

S.FORM: mana nu djumaken itjen," aya tja mareqali.
S.PHON: mana nu ɉumakən icən," aja ca marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: mana nu djumaken itjen," aya tja mareqali.
S.PHON: mana nu ɉumakən iʦən," aja ʦa marəqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: in case we’re found out.”

S.FORM: manu inika makaya sema teku a maɫeḍusa.
S.PHON: manu inika makaja səma təku a maʎəɖusa.
S.FORM: manu inika makaya sema teku a maljedrusa.
S.PHON: manu inika makaja səma təku a maʎəɖusa.
S.TRANSL: But the two of them couldn’t get down.

S.FORM: "kemuda itjen, qalian?" aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: "kəmuda icən, qaɭian" aja tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: "kemuda itjen, qalian?" aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: "kəmuda iʦən, qaɭian" aja tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: They said: “What shall we do, friend?”

S.FORM: matsingul anga sakamaya i vavaw ta kamaya.
S.PHON: maʦiŋuɭ aŋa sakamaja i vavaw ta kamaja.
S.FORM: macingul anga sakamaya i vavaw ta kamaya.
S.PHON: maʦiŋuɭ aŋa sakamaja i vavaw ta kamaja.
S.TRANSL: They were just stranded up the mango tree,

S.FORM: qemaung aza mareqali.
S.PHON: qəmauŋ aza marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: qemaung aza mareqali.
S.PHON: qəmauŋ aza marəqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: and they cried.

S.FORM: ka inika makayanga sema teku, maselem anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: ka inika makajaŋa səma təku, masəɭəm aŋa sakamaja.
S.FORM: ka inika makayanga sema teku, maselem anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: ka inika makajaŋa səma təku, masəɭəm aŋa sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: They couldn’t get down, and it got dark.

S.FORM: kipakiman anga na matjaɫaɫak.
S.PHON: kipakiman aŋa na macaʎaʎak.
S.FORM: kipakiman anga na matjaljaljak.
S.PHON: kipakiman aŋa na maʦaʎaʎak.
S.TRANSL: Their parents looked for them.

S.FORM: manu patadaɫan naza mareqali aza kina kata kama a namasisapuy.
S.PHON: manu patadaʎan naza marəqaɭi aza kina kata kama a namasisapuj.
S.FORM: manu patadaljan naza mareqali aza kina kata kama a namasisapuy.
S.PHON: manu patadaʎan naza marəqaɭi aza kina kata kama a namasisapuj.
S.TRANSL: The friends could see their mothers and fathers holding torches.

S.FORM: "ɫa qunu ɫa qunu, inu mun?" aya za kama.
S.PHON: "ʎa qunu ʎa qunu, inu mun" aja za kama.
S.FORM: "lja qunu lja qunu, inu mun?" aya za kama.
S.PHON: "ʎa qunu ʎa qunu, inu mun" aja za kama.
S.TRANSL: The father(s) said: “Children children, where are you?”

S.FORM: tjaɫu tjai teku tjayamadju.
S.PHON: caʎu cai təku cajamaɉu.
S.FORM: tjalju tjai teku tjayamadju.
S.PHON: ʦaʎu ʦai təku ʦajamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: They came right below them.

S.FORM: "aki pasainu a mareka tja aɫak?
S.PHON: "aki pasainu a marəka ca aʎak
S.FORM: "aki pasainu a mareka tja aljak?
S.PHON: "aki pasainu a marəka ʦa aʎak
S.TRANSL: “Where have our children gone to?

S.FORM: aki matsay?
S.PHON: aki maʦaj
S.FORM: aki macay?
S.PHON: aki maʦaj
S.TRANSL: Are they dead?

S.FORM: aki semainu?" aya za matjaɫaɫak na mareqali.
S.PHON: aki səmainu" aja za macaʎaʎak na marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: aki semainu?" aya za matjaljaljak na mareqali.
S.PHON: aki səmainu" aja za maʦaʎaʎak na marəqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: Where have they gone?” the friends’ parents said.

S.FORM: naqeɫetep aza mareqali taza rekutjan taza matjaɫaɫak.
S.PHON: naqəʎətəp aza marəqaɭi taza rəkucan taza macaʎaʎak.
S.FORM: naqeljetep aza mareqali taza rekutjan taza matjaljaljak.
S.PHON: naqəʎətəp aza marəqaɭi taza rəkuʦan taza maʦaʎaʎak.
S.TRANSL: The friends kept quiet for fear of their parents.

S.FORM: manu vaik a sema umaq aza matjaɫaɫak.
S.PHON: manu vaik a səma umaq aza macaʎaʎak.
S.FORM: manu vaik a sema umaq aza matjaljaljak.
S.PHON: manu vaik a səma umaq aza maʦaʎaʎak.
S.TRANSL: So the parents went home.

S.FORM: ka zemuker i umaq, pasemalaw taza i umaq: "neka tia mareqali; matsay kaumaya," aya taza taqaɫaqaɫan.
S.PHON: ka zəmukər i umaq, pasəmaɭaw taza i umaq: "nəka tia marəqaɭi; maʦaj kaumaja," aja taza taqaʎaqaʎan.
S.FORM: ka zemuker i umaq, pasemalaw taza i umaq: "neka tia mareqali; macay kaumaya," aya taza taqaljaqaljan.
S.PHON: ka zəmukər i umaq, pasəmaɭaw taza i umaq: "nəka tia marəqaɭi; maʦaj kaumaja," aja taza taqaʎaqaʎan.
S.TRANSL: When they got home they told the people there: “There is no sign of the friends; perhaps they are dead,” they said to their fellow-villagers.

S.FORM: pai ka maɫianga vaik anga kemim aza taqaɫaqaɫan.
S.PHON: pai ka maʎiaŋa vaik aŋa kəmim aza taqaʎaqaʎan.
S.FORM: pai ka maljianga vaik anga kemim aza taqaljaqaljan.
S.PHON: pai ka maʎiaŋa vaik aŋa kəmim aza taqaʎaqaʎan.
S.TRANSL: So next day the villagers went to look for them,

S.FORM: manu patsun aza maḍusa.
S.PHON: manu paʦun aza maɖusa.
S.FORM: manu pacun aza madrusa.
S.PHON: manu paʦun aza maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: and two of them saw the children (or and the two (children) saw them coming).

S.FORM: "uzai a ɫavingan i vavaw ta kamaya," aya za qaliqali.
S.PHON: "uzai a ʎaviŋan i vavaw ta kamaja," aja za qaɭiqaɭi.
S.FORM: "uzai a ljavingan i vavaw ta kamaya," aya za qaliqali.
S.PHON: "uzai a ʎaviŋan i vavaw ta kamaja," aja za qaɭiqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: These others said: “There’s some monkeys up a mango tree.

S.FORM: "ari tja kuangaw," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: "ari ca kuaŋaw," aja tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: "ari tja kuangaw," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: "ari ʦa kuaŋaw," aja tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: Let’s shoot them.”

S.FORM: ka zukeren, mavan aza maḍusa.
S.PHON: ka zukərən, mavan aza maɖusa.
S.FORM: ka zukeren, mavan aza madrusa.
S.PHON: ka zukərən, mavan aza maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: When they got there, they saw it was the two children.

S.FORM: "pindjek sa suledjeledji.
S.PHON: "pinɉək sa suɭəɉəɭəɉi.
S.FORM: "pindjek sa suledjeledji.
S.PHON: "pinɉək sa suɭəɉəɭəɉi.
S.TRANSL: They said to the two friends: “You silly fools! (?)

S.FORM: aki manu ɫavingan, aya men tjanumun.
S.PHON: aki manu ʎaviŋan, aja mən canumun.
S.FORM: aki manu ljavingan, aya men tjanumun.
S.PHON: aki manu ʎaviŋan, aja mən ʦanumun.
S.TRANSL: We thought you were monkeys.

S.FORM: uzi kemikim amen a taqaɫaqaɫan tjanumun.
S.PHON: uzi kəmikim amən a taqaʎaqaʎan canumun.
S.FORM: uzi kemikim amen a taqaljaqaljan tjanumun.
S.PHON: uzi kəmikim amən a taqaʎaqaʎan ʦanumun.
S.TRANSL: All the village is searching for you;

S.FORM: manu i vavaw mun tusa kamaya.
S.PHON: manu i vavaw mun tusa kamaja.
S.FORM: manu i vavaw mun tusa kamaya.
S.PHON: manu i vavaw mun tusa kamaja.
S.TRANSL: and here you are up a mango tree.

S.FORM: keɫu sateku," ayain aza mareqali.
S.PHON: kəʎu satəku," ajain aza marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: kelju sateku," ayain aza mareqali.
S.PHON: kəʎu satəku," ajain aza marəqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: Come on down.”

S.FORM: "inika men a makaya," aya za mareqali.
S.PHON: "inika mən a makaja," aja za marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: "inika men a makaya," aya za mareqali.
S.PHON: "inika mən a makaja," aja za marəqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: The friends said: “We can’t get down.”

S.FORM: sisan tjekezanga, sa sateku anga.
S.PHON: sisan cəkəzaŋa, sa satəku aŋa.
S.FORM: sisan tjekezanga, sa sateku anga.
S.PHON: sisan ʦəkəzaŋa, sa satəku aŋa.
S.TRANSL: A ladder was made for them, and they got down. (Moral: We mustn’t be greedy or steal.)
